title: Argparse Interface, Help, Options, and Usage Errors · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in testable user-facing command interface and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.
Reject invalid ranges, conflicting flags, unsupported choices, and missing values at the parser boundary with usage evidence.
Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Argument Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Bookbot team handles case 3525 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to define and validate a reproducible task-oriented argparse interface with useful help; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Python314 Argparse.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Python314 Argparse into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Python314 Argparse, keep Python314 Argparse connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-python314-argparse-1aomqi
# Competency: bookbot-python314-argparse.
# Case 2713: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_python314_argparse_1aomqi(arguments: int = 6, invalid_arguments: int = 3, invalid_rejected: int = 3, help_has_units: bool = True, abbreviations_disabled: bool = True, color_controlled: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1aomqi = "1aomqi-iqmoa1-2713"
    assert evidence_variant_1aomqi
    evidence_axes_1aomqi = (
        "corpus1aomqi",
        "policyiqmoa1",
        "revision2713",
        "command1aomqi2713",
        "reportiqmoa12713",
        "test27131aomqi",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1aomqi)) == 6
    if arguments < 1 or invalid_arguments < 0:
        return False, arguments, "argument-shape"
    if invalid_rejected != invalid_arguments:
        return False, invalid_rejected, "argument-validation"
    if not help_has_units:
        return False, arguments, "help-contract"
    if not abbreviations_disabled or not color_controlled:
        return False, arguments, "parser-reproducibility"
    assert invalid_rejected == invalid_arguments
    return True, arguments, "testable-cli"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Python314 Argparse structural check is only one gate. testable user-facing command interface must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
